title: Humboltia cochlearis
intro: Humboldtia cochlearis, also known as the 'ear shell tree', is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia cochlearis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat infections, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Humboltia cochlearis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Humboltia, which is a group of flowering plants. The classification of Humboltia cochlearis is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Gentianales, family - Gentianaceae, genus - Humboltia, species - H. cochlearis. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but this is the most commonly accepted classification. Further research may provide more detailed information about the classification of this species.

side_effects: Humboldtia cochlearis is known to be highly toxic and has been reported to have several side effects.
